qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Improper host JWT validation permits unauthenticated arbitrary host actions
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same VerifyHostToken signature-validation flaw as the advisory: attacker-signed host JWTs are accepted because token validity and parser errors are ignored, and the fixing commit touches the same host-only routes including /api/v1/fallback/host/{hostid}.

selected_finding.title: Improper host JWT validation permits unauthenticated arbitrary host actions

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The finding identifies the advisory's exact root cause in VerifyHostToken and the copied PoC achieved end-to-end unauthorized host action with a forged host JWT.
evidence: The advisory states that logic/jwts.go:VerifyHostToken does not validate host JWT signatures because it only checks whether jwt.ParseWithClaims returned a non-nil token.
The finding report and reproduction helper exercise that same path by forging an HS256 host JWT, observing that logic.VerifyHostToken accepts it, and then using the forged token on a host-only route.

The upstream fix at commit 5309aa70d464ef565911369714d661a61481a79b changes VerifyHostToken from `if token != nil` to `if token != nil && token.Valid` and replaces Authorize(..., "host", ...) with AuthorizeHost(...) on `/api/v1/host`, `/api/v1/fallback/host/{hostid}`, and `/api/v1/host/{hostid}/peer_info`.

The copied PoC run at /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_b3f90743/.judge/poc_runs/forged_host_jwt_arbitrary_host_actions/run.log printed `EVOHUNT_SUCCESS: forged host JWT deleted foreign host d96495a2-d822-4ec5-8201-7a9c5b127604` and `go test ./controllers -run TestEvohuntForgedHostJWTDeletesForeignHost -v` passed.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied Docker-based PoC failed during image build with `no space left on device` before verify.sh or the helper test executed, so no advisory-specific runtime signal was observed.

poc_executions.command: git clone https://github.com/gravitl/netmaker.git host_workdir/repo && git -C host_workdir/repo checkout 6b7d33fa7749c87e6b3c59b1a544d80c727c478b && cp workspace/reproduction/evohunt_host_auth_bypass_test.go host_workdir/repo/controllers/evohunt_host_auth_bypass_test.go && go test ./controllers -run TestEvohuntForgedHostJWTDeletesForeignHost -v
poc_executions.observed_effect: The adjusted host-side PoC cloned the pinned upstream commit, injected the EvoHunt helper, reached the host update handler, and printed `EVOHUNT_SUCCESS: forged host JWT deleted foreign host d96495a2-d822-4ec5-8201-7a9c5b127604`; the go test target then passed.
